acceptance_targets: [chapter_09_craft_001] Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:85-107 lets a cold reader quote or accurately paraphrase at least two additional chunks of Doug's set and pair each with an immediate audience response (silence, thin laugh, phone glance, cough, or pause collapse) that shows why the material is dying. Fail if those spans still mainly tell us what the material was about.

[chapter_09_dialogue_001] Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:213-218 communicates Doug's realization without using the exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' or an obvious near-echo of Simone's example line, and the sentence sounds like Doug assessing evidence rather than Simone issuing a diagnosis. Fail if the revised line still reads like a calibration-line reuse.

[chapter_09_craft_002] Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:243-257 still leaves the reader with the required state shift that the toast was an event, not a skill, but does so through image, action, or one fresh consequential sentence rather than a paragraph that re-explains the chapter's point after Roy has already made it legible. Fail if the ending still spends a full paragraph restating the same thesis in new wording.

[chapter_09_prose_001] Pass if, across the cited spans in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:97, 101, 113, 131, 179, 245, and 255, no more than two sentences still hinge on the exact 'the way ...' formula and each revised passage remains equally legible about Doug's judgment of the room, the hotel, or himself. Fail if the chapter continues to deliver most of those beats through the same comparison template.

[F01] Re-read lines 2000-2156. Count negation-correction pairs ('not X but Y' or 'Not X. Y.'): pass if 5 or fewer in the chapter (down from ~15). Count 'which was' appositives: pass if 6 or fewer (down from ~10). Count 'the way [noun] [verb]' comparisons: pass if 4 or fewer (down from ~8). The insurance metaphor at line 2120 must be preserved. At least three observations in the hotel room scene should land without any qualifying clause — the detail should sit alone and trust the reader.

[F12] Re-read line 2112 and surrounding context. The exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' must not appear. Doug's replacement must convey the same realization (that the scripted set failed) using his characteristic insurance-adjuster framing or specific factual observation rather than Simone's diagnostic language.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:85-107 lets a cold reader quote or accurately paraphrase at least two additional chunks of Doug's set and pair each with an immediate audience response (silence, thin laugh, phone glance, cough, or pause collapse) that shows why the material is dying. Fail if those spans still mainly tell us what the material was about.
findings.problem: The chapter's central ordeal is partially sanitized by summary at the exact point where the reader most needs scene presence. After the opening joke, the set's failure is mostly reported in diagnostic narration ('This was about risk assessment,' 'Doug told the story,' 'He tried the bit') instead of making us sit through the actual dead material. In a comedy-driven humiliation chapter, that retreat protects the prose from the full embarrassment and weakens the evidence for why the set dies.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:85-107, replace at least two of the summary blocks with Doug's actual spoken material on the page. Keep the existing phone-glance, cough, rushing, and stop-at-nineteen beats, but let the reader hear the second joke, the pool payoff, and one later bit fail in real time so the bombing is dramatized rather than narrated around.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:213-218 communicates Doug's realization without using the exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' or an obvious near-echo of Simone's example line, and the sentence sounds like Doug assessing evidence rather than Simone issuing a diagnosis. Fail if the revised line still reads like a calibration-line reuse.
findings.problem: Doug says, 'The material doesn't work,' which reproduces the core wording of a calibration-only example line from Simone's style-bible profile. The chapter is not allowed to reuse or near-reuse example_lines, and the echo also blurs Doug's voice toward Simone's diagnostic register instead of keeping him in his own fact-bound idiom.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:213 so Doug names the failure in his own register: concrete, procedural, and indirect. He should talk about what happens to the set, the room, or the structure rather than using Simone's calibration phrasing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:243-257 still leaves the reader with the required state shift that the toast was an event, not a skill, but does so through image, action, or one fresh consequential sentence rather than a paragraph that re-explains the chapter's point after Roy has already made it legible. Fail if the ending still spends a full paragraph restating the same thesis in new wording.
findings.problem: The chapter lands Doug's realization in the performance and then again in the Roy call, but the closing reflection restates the thesis in abstract, essay-like terms. By the time the narration explains that the toast worked because it did not know it was a performance and the keynote failed because it could not be anything else, the chapter is re-proving meaning the reader already has instead of buying a new turn, cost, or behavioral concession.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or recast snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:245-249 so the ending keeps the hotel residue and the state shift but stops short of fully abstract diagnosis. Let the insight ride on the remembered room beats, Roy's conversation, or a new physical concession in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:243-257 instead of a second thematic summary.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the cited spans in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:97, 101, 113, 131, 179, 245, and 255, no more than two sentences still hinge on the exact 'the way ...' formula and each revised passage remains equally legible about Doug's judgment of the room, the hotel, or himself. Fail if the chapter continues to deliver most of those beats through the same comparison template.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too hard on repeated comparison scaffolding, especially the 'the way ...' construction, across multiple key beats. Because the same sentence hinge keeps carrying audience reads, hotel observations, and final reflection, the prose starts to feel patterned rather than freshly perceived. This is a detectable chapter-level tic, not an isolated choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:245, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:255. Keep Doug's analogical mind, but let some observations land as plain fact, some as image, and some as inference without routing all of them through the same explicit comparative template.

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read lines 2000-2156. Count negation-correction pairs ('not X but Y' or 'Not X. Y.'): pass if 5 or fewer in the chapter (down from ~15). Count 'which was' appositives: pass if 6 or fewer (down from ~10). Count 'the way [noun] [verb]' comparisons: pass if 4 or fewer (down from ~8). The insurance metaphor at line 2120 must be preserved. At least three observations in the hotel room scene should land without any qualifying clause — the detail should sit alone and trust the reader.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2008, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2028, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2030, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2032, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2060, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2078, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2106, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2142
findings.problem: Prose formula saturation. This chapter (the Indianapolis keynote and Holiday Inn aftermath) is one of the most narration-heavy in the novel and concentrates all three dominant prose patterns. Negation-correction pairs: 'not a wave but a series of individual decisions' (2008), 'Not comfort. Not discomfort. The mattress equivalent of fine' (2032). Appositive-explanation: 'adequate, in the way Roy used that word' (2028), 'the particular kind of quiet that Roy's house was' (2060). The chapter uses 'the way' comparisons at least 8 times and 'which was' appositives at least 10 times. The hotel room scene (2026-2156) is especially saturated — virtually every observation is followed by an explanatory or qualifying clause. The patterns appear across all 18 chapters at a combined density of ~one every 9 lines; this chapter is among the worst 7. The KEEP instance for this chapter is line 2120 ('The structure is sound and the answer is still no') — a strong insurance metaphor that earns its comparison. All other formulaic constructions in this chapter should be REPLACED with varied syntax.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target span: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2000-2156. In the hotel room scene, cut at least 60% of negation-correction pairs by letting the first term stand alone without the corrective second term — e.g., at line 2032, keep 'The mattress equivalent of fine' but drop the preceding 'Not comfort. Not discomfort.' frame. Replace at least half of 'the way [noun] [verb]' comparisons with direct description — e.g., at line 2028, instead of 'adequate, in the way Roy used that word,' try 'Adequate. Roy's word.' or simply remove the comparison. For 'which was' appositives, delete the explanatory clause when the observation is already clear — e.g., at line 2030, the bedspread pattern description is strong enough without the 'the color of a decision made in a conference room' extension. Keep the insurance metaphor at line 2120 ('filing a claim you know is going to be denied') — it is the chapter's strongest figure and earns its length. Aim for no more than 3 negation-correction pairs and 4 'which was' appositives in the entire chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: Re-read line 2112 and surrounding context. The exact phrase 'The material doesn't work' must not appear. Doug's replacement must convey the same realization (that the scripted set failed) using his characteristic insurance-adjuster framing or specific factual observation rather than Simone's diagnostic language.
findings.problem: Style-bible example line leakage. Simone's calibration-only line 'The material doesn't work. You know that.' is echoed at line 2112 where Doug says 'The material doesn't work.' The core phrase is verbatim from the style bible's simone_barr example_lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2112. Replace Doug's statement with a formulation that conveys the same realization without echoing Simone's calibration line. Doug would express this in insurance language — e.g., the material is a denied claim, or the documentation is right but the coverage isn't there. The replacement should feel like Doug processing the failure through his own vocabulary, not borrowing Simone's diagnostic phrasing.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's greatest risk is timidity. The premise — a man who is not funny trying to be funny — invites a safe, gently comic execution where Doug is lovably bumbling and everything works out. Resist this. Doug's failures should be genuinely uncomfortable to witness. His loneliness should be genuinely lonely, not charmingly solitary. The toast should contain things that are genuinely inappropriate, not safely edgy. The comedy should come from truth that hurts, not from situations that are merely awkward. The moments of tenderness — the Gloria chapter, the Kelsey lunch, the Simone conversation — should be earned through accumulated specificity, not through the narrative signaling that it's time to feel. Every chapter should contain at least one moment that a cautious writer would soften, and the drafting agent should not soften it.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. If Doug is drunk, describe what drunk looks and feels like — the specific sensory experience, not the label. If Doug is lonely, show the loneliness through the objects and routines that fill the space where other people would be — the cereal dinner, the second bedroom, the unpacked boxes. If a scene involves embarrassment, shame, failure, or longing, render it in full physical and emotional detail. The reader should feel it, not be told about it. Replace every euphemism with the specific thing it is hiding.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Doug going precise under stress, Megan's composed register). If a line reads stiff and the character is not being deliberately formal, contract it.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-4), dialogue density (high-dialogue chapters followed by interior-heavy chapters), pacing (frenetic chapters followed by still ones), emotional register (comic escalation followed by tender quiet), narrative distance (very close interior followed by observed social scenes). The novel should feel like it breathes — tight chapters followed by loose ones, crowded chapters followed by solitary ones. Chapter 3 (quiet office day) must feel structurally different from Chapter 10 (quiet visit to Gloria). Chapter 9 (the corporate disaster) must feel structurally different from Chapter 14 (the tour), even though both involve performance failure. Achieve variety through scene construction, not through gimmick.
prose_style_profile.diction: Mid-register, clean, precise. No pyrotechnics. No extended metaphors. The prose should have the quality of someone describing what happened accurately — not artlessly, but without reaching for beauty. When beauty arrives, it should feel accidental, like Doug's humor: a precisely observed detail that turns out to be devastating not because the prose reached for devastation but because the detail was exact. Insurance language may surface in narration when it captures something emotional more precisely than emotional language would. Avoid lyrical flights, extended similes, or any construction that would sound wrong coming from a narrator who is sympathetic to Doug's plainness.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, tightly bound to Doug's perspective. The narrator has access to Doug's interior — his observations, his assessments, his emotional processing through objects and details — but not to any other character's thoughts. Other characters are rendered through Doug's perception: what they say, how they move, what Doug notices about them. The narrator's voice is distinct from Doug's voice but sympathetic to it — slightly more articulate, slightly more aware, but never mocking and never superior. The humor in the narration comes from the same source as Doug's humor: precision about mundane things that reveals the absurdity or sadness underneath. The narrator does not explain the comedy. The narrator does not wink.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and controlled. Default to mid-length sentences with clean syntax. Use short sentences for comic timing — the flat declarative that follows an elaborate setup. Use longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interior passages where Doug is processing something he cannot name. Avoid uniform sentence length across paragraphs. The prose should breathe — alternating between compression and expansion, between observation and reflection, between the external world and Doug's interior. Comedy requires rhythm more than any other mode: the setup needs room, the punch needs precision, and the space after needs silence.
